steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content:
The equation $2x(x-3)(x+5)=0$ is written as a product equal to zero. By the Zero Product Property, at least one factor must equal zero. Since the constant 2 never equals zero, we set each remaining factor to zero:

$$x = 0$$
$$x - 3 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 3$$
$$x + 5 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -5$$

steps.title: Match a solution to the choices
steps.content: The three solutions are \$0$, \$3$, and $-5$. Among the given choices, only \$0$ is listed. That matches choice B — B is correct.
